'''Oliver Wilson''' (born 22 March 2000) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] and the [[2019 RFL Championship|Championship]], and spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[2020 RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Wilson was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England.<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/who-is-oliver-wilson-meet-the-highly-rated-prop-wigan-warriors-have-just-signed-5396152 Who is Oliver Wilson? Meet the highly-rated prop Wigan Warriors have just signed]</ref>

==Playing career==
===Bradford Bulls===
Between 2018 and 2019 he played for the [[Bradford Bulls]].

===Huddersfield Giants===
Wilson left Bradford and became a part of Huddersfield's academy team, eventually becoming a first team regular.

Wilson has spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

In 2019, he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921783|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils v Huddersfield Giants|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

On 28 May 2022, he played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809/|title=Challenge Cup player ratings as numerous stars shine in enthralling contest|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|access-date=2022-05-29|archive-date=2022-06-03|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220603014903/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Wilson played 20 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com|date=29 September 2023 }}</ref>

Wilson played 17 games for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 26 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=26 May 2021|accessdate=27 May 2021|title=Huddersfield Giants sextet set for Championship loan moves|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-loan-championship-rugby-20683895.amp}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
On 10 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] on a 4-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=10 Nov 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Huddersfield Giants forward Oliver Wilson signs for Wigan Warriors|url=https://www.totalrl.com/huddersfield-giants-forward-oliver-wilson-signs-for-wigan-warriors/}}</ref>

===International ===
He made his {{rlnt|England}} début on 29 June 2024 v {{rlnt|France}} in Toulouse.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=29 June 2024|title=France v England|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4465137}}</ref>
